2605.30208.title: Automating Low-Risk Code Review at Meta: RADAR, Risk Calibration, and Review Efficiency
2605.30208.paper_summary: AI-assisted coding tools have altered software production. At Meta, significant lines of code per human-landed diff grew by 105.9% year over year and per-developer diff volume rose 51%, with agentic AI responsible for over 80% of that growth. Meanwhile, the share of diffs receiving timely review has declined, exposing a widening gap between code supply and reviewer bandwidth. We ask three questions that progress from feasibility through calibration to impact: (1) can risk-stratified automation operate at scale across diverse organizations, (2) how does tuning the risk threshold affect the trade-off between automation yield and safety, and (3) to what extent does automated review reduce end-to-end latency for AI-generated changes? We deployed RADAR (Risk Aware Diff Auto Review), a multi-stage funnel that classifies each diff by authorship and source type, applies eligibility gates, static heuristics, a machine-learned Diff Risk Score, LLM-based Automated Code Review, and deterministic validation before landing qualifying changes. We evaluate RADAR through telemetry covering 535K+ RADAR-reviewed diffs, observational before-after comparisons for policy changes, and difference-in-differences analysis of efficiency outcomes. RADAR has reviewed 535K+ diffs and landed 331K+. Relaxing the Diff Risk Score threshold from the 25th to the 50th percentile increased the approve rate to 60.31%. The revert rate for RADAR-reviewed diffs is 1/3 that of non-RADAR diffs, and the Production Incident rate is 1/50 that of non-RADAR diffs. RADAR reduces median time to close by over 330% and median diff review wall time by 35%. Risk-aware layered automation can materially reduce review bottlenecks created by AI-driven code growth without compromising production safety.
2605.30208.paper_summary_zh: AI輔助編碼工具已經改變了軟體生產。在Meta，每位人員的代碼行數增長了105.9%，每位開發者的差異量上升了51%，而代理AI負責了超過80%的增長。與此同時，及時審查的差異比例下降，暴露了代碼供應和審查人員帶寬之間日益擴大的差距。我們提出三個問題，從可行性到校準再到影響進行探討：(1) 風險分層自動化能否在不同組織中大規模運作，(2) 調整風險閾值如何影響自動化產出與安全之間的權衡，以及(3) 自動化審查在多大程度上減少AI生成變更的端到端延遲？我們部署了RADAR（風險感知差異自動審查），這是一個多階段漏斗，根據作者和來源類型對每個差異進行分類，應用資格門檻、靜態啟發式、機器學習的差異風險分數、基於LLM的自動代碼審查，以及確定性驗證，然後再落實合格的變更。我們通過涵蓋535K+ RADAR審查差異的遙測、政策變更的觀察性前後比較，以及效率結果的差異中的差異分析來評估RADAR。RADAR已經審查了535K+差異並落實了331K+。將差異風險分數閾值從第25百分位放寬到第50百分位，批准率提高至60.31%。RADAR審查的差異的回退率是非RADAR差異的1/3，而生產事件率是非RADAR差異的1/50。RADAR將中位數關閉時間減少了超過330%，中位數差異審查牆時間減少了35%。風險感知的分層自動化可以實質性減少由AI驅動的代碼增長所造成的審查瓶頸，而不會妥協生產安全。
2605.30208.author: Chris Adams et.al.
2605.30208.authors: Chris Adams, Arjun Singh Banga, Parveen Bansal, Souvik Bhattacharya, Rujin Cao, Pedro Canahuati, Nate Cook, Brian Ellis, Prabhakar Goyal, Gurinder Grewal, Tianyu He, Matt Labunka, Alex Manners, David Molnar, Ging Cee Ng, Vishal Parekh, Jiefu Pei, Frederic Sagnes, James Saindon, Will Shackleton, Sid Sidhu, Gursharan Singh, Karthik Chengayan Sridhar, Matt Steiner, Pratibha Udmalpet, Sean Xia, Stacey Yan, Audris Mockus, Peter Rigby, Nachiappan Nagappan
